content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
August 30,
1993
Jimmy R.
Lewis
Director
Assembly Office of Research
1020
N Street,
Suite 408
Sacramento, California
95814
Re:
Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File.No.
I-93-208a
Dear Mr.
Lewis:
The purpose of this letter is to provide you follow-up advice
with respect to your request for informal assistance on behalf of
Brent Barnhart regarding the conflict-of-interest and other
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the Act”).
1
According to your facts,
Mr. Barnhart is a former employee of
Blue Cross.
Regulation 18704 provides:
Source of income,
as used in Government Code
Section 87103(c), shall not include a former
employer if:
All income from the employer was
received by or accrued to the public official prior
to the time he or she became a public official; the
income was received in the normal course of the
previous employment;
and there was no expectation
by the public official at the time he or she
assumed office of renewed employment with the
former employer.
1
Government Code Sections 81000—91015.
All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission
regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000
q.
All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
428 J Street, Suite 800 •
P.O. Box 807
I
Sacramento, CA
95812 I
(916) 322-5660


Our File No.
I-93--208a
Page
2
Thus, the Blue Cross would not be considered a “source of
income” under the Act where the provisions of Regulation 18704
apply.
If you have any questions, please feel free to contact met at
(916)
322—5901.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
Counsel,
Legal Division

sections.question_synthetic: Whether a former employer (Blue Cross) should be considered a 'source of income' for purposes of the Political Reform Act's conflict-of-interest provisions when a public official (Brent Barnhart) worked there before assuming office.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Blue Cross would not be considered a 'source of income' under the Act if the conditions in Regulation 18704 are met: all income was received prior to becoming a public official, it was received in the normal course of previous employment, and there was no expectation of renewed employment at the time of assuming office.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION section headers present; document is formatted as a letter with discussion of regulatory provisions. Question and conclusion inferred from letter content. File number format (I-93-208a) indicates informal advice.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether a former employer (Blue Cross) should be considered a 'source of income' for purposes of the Political Reform Act's conflict-of-interest provisions when a public official (Brent Barnhart) worked there before assuming office.

CONCLUSION: Blue Cross would not be considered a 'source of income' under the Act if the conditions in Regulation 18704 are met: all income was received prior to becoming a public official, it was received in the normal course of previous employment, and there was no expectation of renewed employment at the time of assuming office.
embedding.first_500_words: California Fair Political Practices Commission August 30, 1993 Jimmy R. Lewis Director Assembly Office of Research 1020 N Street, Suite 408 Sacramento, California 95814 Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance Our File.No. I-93-208a Dear Mr. Lewis: The purpose of this letter is to provide you follow-up advice with respect to your request for informal assistance on behalf of Brent Barnhart regarding the conflict-of-interest and other provisions of the Political Reform Act (the Act”). 1 According to your facts, Mr. Barnhart is a former employee of Blue Cross. Regulation 18704 provides: Source of income, as used in Government Code Section 87103(c), shall not include a former employer if: All income from the employer was received by or accrued to the public official prior to the time he or she became a public official; the income was received in the normal course of the previous employment; and there was no expectation by the public official at the time he or she assumed office of renewed employment with the former employer. 1 Government Code Sections 81000—91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000 q. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 I Sacramento, CA 95812 I (916) 322-5660 Our File No. I-93--208a Page 2 Thus, the Blue Cross would not be considered a “source of income” under the Act where the provisions of Regulation 18704 apply. If you have any questions, please feel free to contact met at (916) 322—5901. Sincerely, Luisa Menchaca Counsel, Legal Division
embedding.summary: FPPC informal advice letter responding to a request regarding whether former employer Blue Cross should be treated as a 'source of income' under the Political Reform Act for public official Brent Barnhart. The FPPC concludes that Regulation 18704 provides an exception excluding former employers from the definition of 'source of income' when specified conditions are met.
